evidence_quotes.quote: the video game industry with the live service game model should be considered a prime exemple of abusive commercial practice on digital practices.

- Micro-transaction : if this practice is not in itself predatory, the aggressive marketing and the fear of missing out (a known psychological bias often used in marketing) especially in game where minor can play encourage addiction.

Unfair Clause : in the user agreement, companies like Ubisoft, a major player in the video-game industry can unilateraly decide to end service and effectively delete a game without any compensation for the user.
Even if one has just bought a User licence and not the game itself, it should be considered abusive due to the absence of suitable compensation.

paragraphs.text: the video game industry with the live service game model should be considered a prime exemple of abusive commercial practice on digital practices. . Two point i would like to adress : - Micro-transaction : if this practice is not in itself predatory, the aggressive marketing and the fear of missing out (a known psychological bias often used in marketing) especially in game where minor can play encourage addiction. Furthermore, it's often included in games that one must first buy at full price. If then again, some micro-transaction can be justified by the continued developpement, there should be a reasonable a middle-ground. - Unfair Clause : in the user agreement, companies like Ubisoft, a major player in the video-game industry can unilateraly decide to end service and effectively delete a game without any compensation for the user. Even if one has just bought a User licence and not the game itself, it should be considered abusive due to the absence of suitable compensation.
